cases.title: Assigned lecturer corrects attendance within edit window and writes audit row
preconditions: Closed ClassSession exists for a section assigned to the acting lecturer
Effective policy manualEditWindow has not elapsed since session close
Enrolled student has an existing AttendanceRecord (e.g. Absent) for the session
Policy mandates a reason for manual corrections
steps: As the assigned lecturer, invoke the attendance correction service for PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status 'Manual Present' and a non-empty reason inside a real DB transaction
Commit the transaction
Read back the AttendanceRecord and the AuditLog rows for the target student/session
cases.expected: AttendanceRecord.status is updated to 'Manual Present' with checkInMethod 'Manual'; exactly one AuditLog row is persisted with actorUserId, timestamp, oldValue (Absent), newValue (Manual Present), and the supplied reason (VR-MC-04, VR-MC-05, DI-07, DI-09)

cases.title: Manual correction transitions Absent to Excused for enrolled student
preconditions: Session is Closed and student was auto-assigned Absent per BR-13
Acting lecturer is assigned to the section and within manualEditWindow
steps: Capture beforeState of AttendanceRecord (status 'Absent')
Apply lecturer correction to status 'Excused' with reason
Capture afterState of AttendanceRecord
cases.expected: beforeState status is 'Absent' and afterState status is 'Excused'; prior rejected CheckInAttempt rows are not deleted; lastModifiedAt is updated

cases.title: PATCH attendance correction returns applied mutation envelope
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to the target section
Correction is within the edit window
steps: Send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with Authorization Bearer, Idempotency-Key header, and body { status: 'Manual Present', reason: '...' }
Inspect HTTP status code and response body
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with the standard envelope { data, meta.requestId, meta.timestamp, error: null } reflecting the applied status 'Manual Present'; error is null

cases.title: Student is denied attendance correction endpoint
preconditions: A Student user is authenticated
A valid session and target student row exist
steps: As a Student, send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with body { status: 'Manual Present', reason: '...' }
Inspect status code, error.code, and any leaked data
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with error.code 'Forbidden'; no AttendanceRecord is mutated and no restricted data is disclosed (VR-MC-01, BR-19)

cases.title: Cross-scope lecturer denied correction on unassigned section
preconditions: Lecturer A is authenticated but is NOT assigned to the section that owns the target session
Correction would otherwise be within the edit window
steps: As Lecturer A, send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} for a section assigned to Lecturer B
Inspect status code and error.code
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with error.code 'OutOfScope'; the AttendanceRecord is unchanged and no audit mutation entry is written (VR-MC-01, BR-14 exception 'deny if section not assigned to lecturer')

cases.title: Lecturer correction after edit window is rejected or escalated
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to the section
Current time is past manualEditWindow from session close
steps: Send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with a valid status and reason after the edit window has elapsed
Inspect status code and error.code
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 409 with error.code 'EditWindowExpired' (or documented escalation-to-admin response per policy); no AttendanceRecord mutation is committed by the lecturer path (VR-MC-03, BR-15)

cases.title: Correction rejected when policy-required reason is missing
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to the section and within the edit window
Effective policy requires a reason for manual corrections
steps: Send PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with a valid status but an empty/missing reason
Inspect status code and error.code
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 400 with error.code 'ReasonRequired'; no mutation and no audit row are written (VR-MC-04)

cases.title: Idempotent replay of correction returns applied mutation without duplicate audit
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned and within the edit window
A correction command has a fixed Idempotency-Key + target attendance row
steps: Submit two parallel PATCH corrections with the same Idempotency-Key, actor, and target attendance row and identical body
Read back the AttendanceRecord and count AuditLog rows for the mutation
cases.expected: Both responses reflect the same already-applied mutation summary; exactly one AttendanceRecord update and exactly one AuditLog entry exist (idempotency validation §5.2, BR-22)

cases.title: Lecturer roster UI applies manual correction with reason and gates save
preconditions: Lecturer is signed in on the desktop roster view for an assigned Closed session within the edit window
Target student row is visible in the roster table
steps: Open the session roster and locate the target student row
Change the attendance status control to 'Manual Present'
Attempt to save with the reason field empty and observe form gating
Enter a reason and save
Observe the updated status badge and roster row
cases.expected: Save is blocked while the reason is empty; after a reason is provided the row status badge updates to 'Manual Present' and a success confirmation is shown, reflecting the persisted correction

cases.title: Lecturer roster UI surfaces edit-window-expired message
preconditions: Lecturer is signed in on the roster view for an assigned session whose manualEditWindow has expired
steps: Open the session roster
Attempt to change a student's attendance status and save
Observe the UI feedback
cases.expected: The UI blocks the save and displays an edit-window-expired message directing the lecturer to the admin override path, consistent with the 409 EditWindowExpired backend behavior (BR-15)
